cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking why Babbage failed to continue his research work in 1828. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand the context. The passage says that Babbage continued his work in London for four years after winning the prize in 1822. Then, his health broke down, and he took a long holiday abroad. When he returned in 1828, he was at the end of his resources, many bills were unpaid, his assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left with expensive tools. For one year no work was done. So the question is asking for the main reason he couldn't continue in 1828. The options are A) poor health, B) ran out of money, C) co-workers argued and left, D) spent all money on bills. First, the passage mentions that when he returned in 1828, he was "at the end of his resources," which suggests financial issues. Also, many bills remained unpaid. His co-workers left, but the question is about the main reason. His chief assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left..."
